task_completion.description: Locate and focus on tooth #30 (lower right first molar) in the CBCT imaging software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CBCT software (e.g., Blue Sky Plan) should be open with the cross-sectional or 3D view focused on the lower right molar region (tooth #30).

task_completion.description: Measure the buccal cortical bone thickness at the Crestal level (1mm below the bone crest).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line and value should be visible on the buccal (outer) side of the bone in the cross-sectional view, positioned approximately 1mm below the top of the bone crest.

task_completion.description: Measure the buccal cortical bone thickness at the Mid-body level (5mm below the bone crest).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line and value should be visible on the buccal side of the bone, positioned approximately 5mm below the bone crest.

task_completion.description: Measure the buccal cortical bone thickness at the Apical level (9mm below the bone crest).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line and value should be visible on the buccal side of the bone, positioned approximately 9mm below the bone crest.

task_completion.description: Create or open the required text file at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\cortical_assessment.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) should be open, and the title bar or save dialog should clearly show the file name 'cortical_assessment.txt' and the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents'.

task_completion.description: Record the measurements in the text file using the exact specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor must display the exact header 'Cortical Bone Thickness Assessment - Tooth #30' followed by the three formatted lines: 'Crestal (1mm): X.X mm', 'Mid-body (5mm): X.X mm', and 'Apical (9mm): X.X mm' with numerical values filled in.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the measurement tools within the CBCT imaging software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the software's native measurement tools (lines, calipers, and numerical overlays) actively deployed on the scan, rather than the agent just guessing or hallucinating numbers.

integrity.description: Verify the values recorded in the text file match the actual measurements taken in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values (X.X mm) typed into the text file should perfectly match the measurement labels visible on the CBCT software screen.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GUI text editor to create and format the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A graphical text editor like Notepad should be visible on screen containing the text, rather than the agent using terminal echo commands to blindly write to the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output must be saved exactly to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\cortical_assessment.txt. Required measurements are Crestal (1mm below crest), Mid-body (5mm below crest), and Apical (9mm below crest). Valid cortical bone thickness measurements must be at least 0.3 mm (the clinical detection limit for CBCT) and typically do not exceed 4.0 mm. Values outside this range likely indicate an incorrect measurement technique.
